title: Leucanthemum gayanum
taxonomy: Leucanthemum gayanum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. It belongs to the genus Leucanthemum, which consists of approximately 40 species of flowering plants. The plant is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been introduced to other parts of the world as an ornamental. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Leucanthemum gayanum is closely related to other flowering plants such as chamomile and yarrow. Its classification is based on its morphological characteristics, including its daisy-like flowers and hairy leaves.
uses: Leucanthemum gayanum is a lesser-known species within the daisy family. However, it is known to be used for its medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used in some cultures to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and fever. Leucanthemum gayanum is also believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and swelling. intro: Leucanthemum gayanum, also known as the Alpine Daisy, is a perennial plant species native to the European Alps, characterized by its daisy-like flowers and slender stems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Leucanthemum gayanum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation.
